observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant Designate–

Trace back details of PPE acquisitions and compute the accumulated depreciation of the CY 2023 reported balance of affected depreciable assets.  Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments upon determination of the correct amounts, for the proper valuation of the PPE;

Draw the necessary adjustments for the recording of errors noted; and

Maintain complete PPE ledger cards/subsidiary records for items of PPE and coordinate with the Property Officer/Inventory Committee for the reconciliation of records, to accurately and reliably present the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

The Property Officer – 
 		
Completely record all the PPEs of the Municipality in the RPCPPE;



Conduct reconciliation of the PPE accounts between the accounting and property records; and


Prepare and maintain Property Cards.


	We further reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to address the problem of reconciliation of PPE items in the accounting and property records, which have become a perennial issue and caused the non-establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not yet traced the details on all PPEs, thus, non-depreciation of some PPEs were still observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant Designate – 

Record adjustments for the accounting errors noted, after verification and supported with documents; and

Refrain from drawing unsupported adjusting journal entries, and henceforth, prepare journal entry vouchers with corresponding supporting documents attached.

The Municipal Treasurer Designate send written notices to the payees at least one month before checks become stale.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not yet made the necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant-Designate –

Exert effort to trace back details of PPE acquisitions, recompute the accumulated depreciation of the CY 2022 reported balance of depreciable assets taking into account the appropriate residual value for each PPE, and the pertinent provisions in COA Circular No. 2015-008 and PAG3.  Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments upon determination of the correct amounts, for the proper valuation of the PPE; and

Maintain complete PPE ledger cards/subsidiary records for items of PPE and coordinate with the Property Officer/Inventory Committee for the identification of recorded assets that were not accounted in the RPCPPE.  Undertake reconciliation of records, to accurately and reliably present the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

The Property Officer–

Compare existing records with the Municipal Accountant-Designate for tracing/identification of PPE which were not accounted in the RPCPPE, and reconcile PPE items and their balances between the accounting and property records;

Prepare and maintain complete Property Cards;


Include real properties in the physical count of PPE; and

Communicate to the Municipal Accountant-Designate the result of the physical count of PPE to update his records.

We further recommended that management conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to address the problem of unidentified PPE items in the accounting records and discrepancies in PPE account balances, which have become a perennial issue and caused the non-establishment of the accuracy of the PPE balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not yet traced the details on all PPEs, thus, non-depreciation of some PPEs were still observed.

observations.recommendation:
1.25	We recommended that:
1.	The Municipal Accountant-
Trace back details of PPE acquisitions, and compute the accumulated depreciation of the CY 2024 reported balance of affected depreciable assets.  Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments upon determination of the correct amounts, for the proper valuation of the PPE;
Maintain complete PPE ledger cards/subsidiary records for items of PPE and coordinate with the Property Officer/Inventory Committee for the reconciliation of records, to accurately and reliably present the PPE accounts in the financial statements; and
Prepare adjusting entries to transfer the items identified costing less than ₱50,000.00 as PPE to the appropriate Semi-Expendable Property Accounts or to the prior period adjustment account, whichever is applicable.
Prepare adjusting entries to transfer the improperly recorded Electrification Project at Apiton Island, amounting to ₱1,579,482.30, from the Power Supply System account to the Other Receivables account.
2.	The GSO Designate:
Completely record all the PPEs of the Municipality in the RPCPPE with corresponding costs;
Conduct reconciliation of the PPE accounts between the accounting and property records; and
Prepare and maintain Property Cards.
1.26	Lastly, we reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that management conduct a One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, following the guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
4.8	We recommended that the LGU remit to the HDMF the identified lacking contributions and loan amortizations for CY 2024, totaling P264,398.59.

5.7	We recommended that the LGU remit to PhilHealth the identified lacking contributions for CY 2024 and prior years, totaling P216,840.94. Henceforth, remit all Due to PhilHealth amounts within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management cause the transfer of the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for the current and prior years to the Special Trust Fund.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate record the reciprocal accounts Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds in the General and Trust Fund books, to recognize the unexpended/unobligated MOOE portion of the LDRRMF in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account in the Trust Fund books.

We also recommended that adequate disclosures of the LDRRMF be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements, in accordance with the template provided in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: The disclosures for LDRRMF made in the CY 2024 Financial Statements does not conform to Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management refrain from granting and releasing advance payments to the contractors without a letter request from the latter requesting the 15% mobilization fee, and require from them the submission of an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee, or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission and confirmed by the Municipality for every request of advance payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant Designate secure a letter request and an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee, or a surety bond callable upon demand from the contractors, before claims for advance payments are processed.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer Designate demand from the contractor G. Builders the corresponding official receipt for the amount of ₱1,445,667.05 mobilization fee paid to the latter, as proof of receipt of payment.

We recommended that the Focal Person of MDRRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer Designate observe the proper accounting and budgetary treatment of the previous years’ unexpended MOOE and CO of the LDRRMF.

We also recommended that the LDRRMC review and update annually the LDRRMFIP and include therein the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant Designate – 

Record adjustments for the restoration of unreleased checks at the end of the year to the cash account, to fairly present the balances of the Cash and Payable accounts at year-end; and

Exert extra effort to identify the noted book reconciling item and secure supporting documents, to reconcile the adjusted cash in bank balance in the BRS with the book balance, and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.

The Municipal Treasurer Designate ensure that the release of checks is properly acknowledged by the payees in the Check Register, with dates of receipt indicated therein, for monitoring.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not yet traced the noted book reconciling items in the BRS

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate submit to the audit team the supporting documents for the recorded Accounts Payable at year-end, to verify the accuracy and validity of the claims; and

We recommended that that the Municipal Accountant Designate transfer the unexpended balances of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund at year-end.
We further recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the detailed list of the PPAs, pertaining to the continuing appropriations of the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to have a regular reconciliation of their records, monitor cancelled, outstanding, unreleased and stale checks, in the recording of collection and deposits and disbursements and other reconciling items so that appropriate adjustments could be made on a monthly basis.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to:

Ensure that all POs/contracts entered into by the Municipal Government incorporates definite terms and conditions of the procured items; and

Establish an internal control mechanism that would ensure that all POs/contracts entered into by Management are duly accomplished and include all the basic information as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010 to protect the interest of the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee, General Service Officer/Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit Physical Inventory Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution, Food Supplies for Distribution, Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment, and the Inventory Cards and Stock Cards for the concerned inventory items and PPEs above, including Inventory Custodian Slip and Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment, where applicable.

We also recommended that adjustments be made on the affected accounts to reflect actual inventory of the above items by preparing a JEV based on physical inventory report, inventory cards and proof of issuance to beneficiaries, if applicable.  And for the procurement of DRR equipment, prepare a JEV to record the recommended AJE.

We recommended that the LCE should require the GSO to insure all Insurable Properties of the LGU the soonest possible time to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified in case of losses due to man-made and catastrophic events.

observations.recommendation:
1.18 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries for
the completed and turned-over projects and record depreciation expense of
the related Property, Plant and Equipment as provided in the above cited
regulations. We further recommend that a Lapsing Schedule for Depreciation
be prepared to serve as a guide in the computation of the annual depreciation.
1.19 Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory
Committee that will lead the conduct of physical inventory of all properties
owned by the municipality and prepare and submit RPCPPE not later than
January 31 of each year.

2.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly disclose in the Notes
to Financial Statements the recorded Investment Property in accordance to the
cited provision in order to give information to the users of financial statements on
the nature of such property.
2.5 The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the disclosures regarding
Investment Property as described on the cited provision.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal
entries to transfer the above items to its proper asset accounts.
3.7 The Municipal Accountant will comply on preparation of adjusting journal entries
for the affected accounts.

4.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to: (i) coordinate with the designated property
officer to reconstruct and reconcile their records, taking into consideration
the accuracy of the beginning inventory balances, the purchases during the year
that were recorded as inventory, the purchases during the year that were
reclassified to expenses, the actual issuances, and other related adjustments,
and prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and (ii) coordinate with the
Inventory Committee and the designated property officer to obtain the
balance of inventory per physical count at the end of the year, reconcile
balances and prepare adjusting entries, if warranted.
b. Designated Property Officer to: (i) prepare regularly the RIS and RSMI and
furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy thereof for recording; and (ii)
reconcile property records with the result of annual physical count and
accounting records.
c. Inventory Committee to: (i) submit CY 2021 RPCI; and (ii) regularly
conduct physical count of inventories and submit a copy of the RPCI to the
Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first
and second semester, respectively.

5.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to ascertain and analyze the transactions where the balances of the
intra-agency accounts rooted and immediately effect necessary correcting
entries in order to present fairly the financial statements.
5.11 The OIC-Municipal Accountant committed to adjust the affected accounts.

6.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive mandate the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to:
a. Ensure that only those fully supported claims shall be recognized as valid
obligations with the documents supporting thereto attached to the journal
vouchers for recording in the books of accounts;
b. Prospectively draw necessary journal entry upon ascertainment that
obligated amount exceeds the actual payment in order not to overstate the
Accounts Payable account;
c. Ensure that classification of accounts is in accordance with the Revised Chart
of Accounts for LGUs;
d. Effect appropriate adjusting entries on the conditions observed; and
e. Maintain complete and detailed subsidiary ledgers for Accounts Payable,
with period aging schedule to aid in the monitoring of the account.

7.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The Municipal Assessor to prepare assessment rolls and furnish copy to the
Municipal Treasurer.
b. The Designated Municipal Treasurer to provide the OIC-Municipal
Accountant, at the beginning of each year, the list of the names of taxpayers
and the amounts due and collectible for the current year for the recording of
the RPT and SET Receivables.
c. The OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that: (a) RPT/SET receivables
accruing to the Municipality are correctly set up in the books based on the
amount due and collectible for the year from the certified list of taxpayers;
and (b) the necessary adjustments on the noted deficiencies be immediately
effected by strictly adhering to the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs.

8.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Maintain cashbooks for cash in bank accounts for all funds, submit
monthly bank statements to support the BRS and general and subsidiary
ledgers for Cash in Bank, Local Currency Current Account of SEF and
Trust Fund in order for the Audit Team to facilitate review, verification
and reconciliation of all records maintained by both the municipal
treasurer and municipal accountant, and to substantiate the reported
account in the financial statement.
b. Have a regular reconciliation of their records, monitor cancelled,
outstanding, unreleased and stale checks, in the recording of collection and
deposits and disbursements and other reconciling items so that
appropriate adjustments could be made on a monthly basis.

9.7 We recommend that that the OIC-Municipal Accountant transfer the
unutilized amount from the LDRRMF to the Trust Liability- DRRM account,
including the Continuing Appropriation for QRF and 70% Mitigation Fund,
if they are unable to identify the specific Capital Outlay PPAs.
9.8 The OIC-Municipal Accountant committed to make the adjusting entries to transfer
the unutilized LDRRM Funds and the reported Continuing Appropriations of the
QRF to the Trust Liability-DRRM account.

10.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain periodically a detailed subsidiary ledger
for the Due to NGAs account as a monitoring tool for the utilization and
liquidation of the fund and the implementation of projects and procurement
activities as well.
10.8 Management committed to comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the General and Subsidiary Ledger to show updated balances of the cash accounts and prepare the corresponding adjustments to the CIB-LCCA account. We further recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain appropriate records, registries and books as basis for the preparation of financial statements of the LGU.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer and all other concerned officials of the LGU to submit the Requisition and Issue Slip and Report on Supplies and Materials Issued for issued/distributed inventory items. Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Supply Ledger Card (SLC) for each type of supplies and other inventory items, to record acquisition, issuance, and disposal.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal General Service Officer to conduct Physical Inventory of all remaining items of goods, supplies, materials including drugs and medicines and medical supplies and equipment in the custody of responsible officials and determine unused or undistributed items. Likewise, require the submission of Stock Cards, Requisition and Issue Slips, Inventory Custodian Slip and such other records/reports supporting issuance of supplies and materials. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the PPE and the unused inventory items by preparing a Journal Entry Voucher to record the PPE accounts in the year and corresponding Ledger Card and the inventory in the corresponding Ledger Card.
